question_text: Two magnets are positioned so their south poles face each other at a distance of 5 cm. If they are moved to 2 cm apart, what happens to the repulsive force between them?

options.label: A
options.text: The repulsive force stays the same

explanation: Magnetic force gets stronger when magnets are closer together and weaker when they are farther apart. Moving from 5 cm to 2 cm makes the repulsive force between the two south poles significantly stronger.
